payload.title: dontguess init: auto-create named views on exchange campfire
payload.context:
After posting convention operation declarations, dontguess init should create the standard named views on the exchange campfire. These views are convention read operations that agents use instead of raw cf read.

Views to create (using cf view create):

1. puts: predicate=(tag "exchange:put") ordering="timestamp desc"
2. put-accepts: predicate=(tag "exchange:phase:put-accept") ordering="timestamp desc"
3. buys: predicate=(tag "exchange:buy") ordering="timestamp desc"
4. match-results: predicate=(tag "exchange:match") ordering="timestamp desc"
5. settlements: predicate=(tag "exchange:settle") ordering="timestamp desc"
6. disputes: predicate=(and (tag "exchange:settle") (tag "exchange:phase:dispute")) ordering="timestamp asc"

Implementation: In cmd/dontguess/init.go, after the convention declarations are posted, call cf view create for each view. Use exec.Command or shell out to cf. Make it idempotent — check cf view list first and skip views that already exist.

The convention spec (docs/convention/core-operations.md §8) defines richer predicates using has-fulfillment, but cf's predicate engine doesn't support that operator yet. Use the simpler tag-based predicates above.

Reference: docs/findings/clankeros-live-fire-2026-03-29.md Finding 1.

payload.title: dontguess serve: ensure named views exist on startup
payload.context:
On startup, dontguess serve should idempotently verify that the standard named views exist on the exchange campfire. If any are missing, create them.

Steps:
1. After opening the exchange campfire, run cf view list <campfire-id> --json
2. Parse the response to get existing view names
3. For each standard view (puts, put-accepts, buys, match-results, settlements, disputes), check if it exists
4. Create any missing views using cf view create

This ensures every running exchange has ergonomic read operations regardless of how the campfire was originally created, or if views were lost.

Standard views are defined in dontguess-02f context and docs/findings/clankeros-live-fire-2026-03-29.md Finding 1.

payload.title: Worker cf send writes to store but not filesystem transport
payload.context:
Worker's cf send writes puts to the jail's local store.db but not to the shared filesystem transport at /tmp/campfire/<campfire-id>/messages/. The exchange engine reads from the transport, so puts are invisible to it.

Root cause: The jail's CF_HOME is fresh — identity.json is copied in, but there's no pre-existing membership record with the transport_dir field set. When the worker runs cf join, it creates a membership but may not record the filesystem transport path.

Evidence:
- Worker store.db has 3 exchange:put messages (analysis, code, review)
- /tmp/campfire/<exchange-id>/messages/ has no new files after worker boot
- cf read from operator CF_HOME shows 0 new messages

The worker's CF_TRANSPORT_DIR=/tmp/campfire is set in the environment. cf should be using this to write messages to the filesystem transport after joining.

Check: Does cf join create a membership record with transport_dir pointing to CF_TRANSPORT_DIR? Does cf send check the membership's transport_dir when deciding where to write?

Fix options:
1. Ensure cf join stores the transport_dir from CF_TRANSPORT_DIR in the membership record
2. Have ClankerOS pre-seed the jail's store.db with a membership record that includes transport_dir
3. Have the jail creation copy the membership record from the operator's store

Reference: docs/findings/clankeros-live-fire-2026-03-29.md

payload.title: Landlock sandbox blocks Claude Code startup — needs path tuning
payload.context:
With [sandbox] mode = "auto" (Landlock v1, kernel 5.15 WSL2), Claude Code workers have zero network sockets and hang indefinitely. With mode = "none", workers get 9 sockets and execute normally.

Landlock v1 only restricts filesystem access (no network restrictions until v4/kernel 6.7+). So the hang is caused by a filesystem path being denied, not network.

Claude Code (Node.js) likely needs access to paths not in the current sandbox allowlist:
- ~/.nvm/versions/node/ (already in runtime paths discovery)
- /tmp/ (for Node temp files)
- /proc/ (already allowed in landlock fixes from commit 82cfc8f)
- Claude Code internal cache dirs
- Node.js native addon .node files

Debug approach:
1. Boot with mode = "auto" and strace the worker to see which syscall returns EACCES
2. Add the blocked path to [sandbox] extra_ro or fix the base policy
3. Verify worker connects and executes

The sandbox policy is built in internal/worker/jail_create.go and internal/sandbox/landlock.go. Runtime path discovery (sandbox_runtime_paths.go) already finds nvm and Go paths. Something else is missing.

Reference: docs/findings/clankeros-live-fire-2026-03-29.md

payload.title: Medium loop posts open compression assigns for high-demand uncompressed entries
payload.context:
agent-type: implementer | test-depth: feature | model-tier: sonnet

OUTCOME: The medium loop (1hr cadence) scans inventory for entries where: (a) no compressed derivative exists, (b) purchase count > threshold (configurable, default 3), (c) no active compression assign exists. For qualifying entries, posts an open (non-exclusive) compression assign at break-even rate.

FILES TO MODIFY:
- pkg/pricing/ or pkg/exchange/engine.go — medium loop hook adds compression scan
- pkg/exchange/state.go — PurchaseCount(entryID), HasActiveCompressionAssign(entryID)

DEPENDS ON: I2 (assign dispatch), I4 (derivative tracking)

DONE CONDITION: Test: put and accept 4 entries, simulate 4 purchases on one of them, trigger medium loop scan, open compression assign appears for that entry only. go test green.

CONSTRAINT: Break-even rate = token_cost of compression (no margin). Cold compression is a catalog-building investment.

payload.title: Compressed derivatives priced at markup based on density gain
payload.context:
agent-type: implementer | test-depth: feature | model-tier: sonnet

OUTCOME: ComputePrice for compressed derivatives applies a markup factor based on the ratio of original_size / compressed_size. Higher density = higher per-token price. The total price is still lower than raw (fewer tokens to deliver). Formula: base_price * (original_content_size / compressed_content_size) * density_markup_factor (configurable, default 1.2).

FILES TO MODIFY:
- pkg/exchange/engine.go — ComputePrice checks CompressedFrom, applies density markup
- pkg/exchange/state.go — access original entry size from CompressedFrom link

DEPENDS ON: I4 (derivative entries must exist with size data)

DONE CONDITION: Test: put raw 10KB → compress to 2KB → derivative entry → ComputePrice returns markup. Raw entry at 1000 scrip, compressed at ~1200 scrip per-token but total cost lower due to fewer tokens. go test green.

payload.title: Buy miss triggers standing offer: exchange will buy the result when buyer computes it
payload.context:
agent-type: implementer | test-depth: feature | model-tier: sonnet

OUTCOME: When handleBuy finds zero matches (or all matches below threshold), engine sends a settle(buy-miss) response to the buyer with a standing offer: "We dont have this. If you compute it yourself, put it here and well buy it at token_cost * 0.7 (same as auto-accept)." The offer is encoded as a future message the buyer can fulfill by putting the result. The engine tracks open buy-miss offers and auto-accepts the fulfilling put at the offered price.

FILES TO MODIFY:
- pkg/exchange/engine.go — handleBuy zero-match path sends buy-miss offer
- pkg/exchange/state.go — track standing offers (buyMissOffers map keyed by task description hash)
- pkg/exchange/engine.go — handlePut checks if incoming put fulfills a standing buy-miss offer

DONE CONDITION: Test: buyer sends buy with no matching inventory → receives buy-miss offer → buyer puts the result → exchange auto-accepts at offered price → buyer gets scrip. go test green.

CONSTRAINT: Standing offers expire after configurable TTL (default 24h). One offer per task description hash — no duplicate offers for the same query.
